items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.description: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
items.titleIt: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: Ethiopia: Washington Announces Visa Restrictions On TPLF Leaders
items.description: [Reporter] The US State Department announced today it will enforce visa restrictions on the leaders of the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) amid a flare in tensions stoking fears about a return to conflict in northern Ethiopia.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: Washington Announces Visa Restrictions On TPLF Leaders

items.title: Ethiopia: Ethiopia Consolidates Continental Leadership in Electric Vehicle Infrastructure
items.description: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia is strengthening its position as Africa's leading adopter of electric vehicles by expanding reliable and resilient charging infrastructure across the country, State Minister of Transport and Logistics Bareo Hassen said.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: Ethiopia Consolidates Continental Leadership in Electric Vehicle Infrastructure

items.title: Africa: Mombasa Declaration Unites 15 Countries in Fight Against Illegal Fishing
items.description: [allAfrica] Mombasa, Kenya -- As illegal, unreported and unregulated (IUU) fishing practices continue to deplete fish populations as well as contribute to human rights abuses at sea, the demand for transparency within the global fishing industry is growing. An estimated one in five fish consumed globally is linked to illegal fishing, a practice often associated with forced labour, human trafficking and environmental destruction. It is estimated that more than 120,000 fishers are currently trapped in situations of modern slavery
items.titleIt: Africa: Mombasa Declaration Unites 15 Countries in Fight Against Illegal Fishing

items.title: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi had assured him of the safe departure of an Italian ship, the Grimaldi Group's car carrier Grande Torino, that has been blocked in the Persian Gulf for months. Araghchi, said Tajani on X, reporting on a phone call with his counterpart, "assured me that in the next few hours the Iranian authorities will ensure the conditions for the safe departure of the Italian Grimaldi ship 'Grande Torino,'" "blocked for months in the Gulf." (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship

items.title: Italy to reopen Tehran embassy on Friday says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani told a question time session in parliament on Wednesday that Italy will reopen its embassy in Tehran on Friday after Iran and the United States reached an agreement to end the war in the Middle East. Premier Giorgia Meloni's government has already said Italy is willing to take part in an international naval mission for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Italy to reopen Tehran embassy on Friday says Tajani

items.title: Remigration is not a 'Christian response' says Pope Leo XIV
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Pope Leo XIV has said that remigration, a policy being proposed by several far-right parties, including former General Roberto Vannacci's new National Future group in Italy, is not a concept that complies with Christian principles. "Many times we do not recognize the reasons why these people had to leave their countries," the American pontiff told reporters outside the papal residence in Castel Gandolf on Tuesday in relation to remigration. "So many reasons: violence, war, conflicts. And so simply saying, 'Let's send them away, so we can wash our hands of the problem' d
items.titleIt: Remigration is not a 'Christian response' says Pope Leo XIV

items.title: Sudan: The European Union Delegation in Sudan - a New Political Engagement or an Attempt to Exert Pressure From Within?
items.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Khartoum / Port Sudan -- A high-level delegation of heads of European Union missions, comprising 14 diplomats and headed by the head of the mission to Sudan, Wolfram Vetter, concluded a visit to Sudan, the first of its kind since the outbreak of war on April 15, 2023. The two-day visit to Khartoum and Port Sudan included a series of meetings with Sudanese officials, including the Chairman of the Sovereignty Council, his deputy, the Prime Minister, and other officials. Discussions focused on several issues related to war and
items.titleIt: Sudan: The European Union Delegation in Sudan - a New Political Engagement or an Attempt to Exert Pressure From Within?
